skill.name: zaker-category-news
skill.description: Fetch category-based trending news from ZAKER including entertainment, technology, finance, sports, and more. Use when the user asks about industry-specific news or trend tracking.
skill.summary: Fetch categorized news from ZAKER across technology, finance, sports, entertainment, and other verticals.
skill.author: ZAKER-coder

security_audit.summary: All 37 static analysis findings are false positives. The two HIGH-severity flags (weak crypto and high entropy) are caused by Chinese text in YAML frontmatter and bilingual content producing elevated byte entropy, respectively. All LOW network findings reference legitimate GET requests to a single known API endpoint (skills.myzaker.com) with no credentials or user input. All MEDIUM external_command findings are markdown documentation examples in fenced code blocks, not executable code. The skill is a straightforward news API client with no malicious patterns.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP GET requests to skills.myzaker.com to fetch news data. All requests use hardcoded parameters with no user input, no authentication credentials, and no data exfiltration risk. This is expected and safe behavior for a news-fetching skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code review confirms only GET requests to a single known API endpoint with hardcoded parameters. No user input, credentials, or exfiltration vectors exist.

content.value_statement: Users need industry-specific news updates without sifting through unrelated content. This skill delivers targeted news from ZAKER across technology, finance, sports, entertainment, and other categories.
actual_capabilities: Fetch trending articles from specific ZAKER news categories
Support nine categories including technology, finance, sports, entertainment, and military
Return 20 articles per request sorted by publish time
Format article output with title, summary, author, and clickable links
Match user intent for both explicit and implicit category requests
limitations: Only supports predefined ZAKER categories, not custom or user-defined topics
Requires internet access to reach the ZAKER API endpoint
Limited to 20 articles per API call

use_cases.title: Industry trend tracking for analysts
use_cases.description: Business analysts monitor specific industry verticals such as technology or finance for emerging trends and market signals. This skill provides regular categorized news updates.

use_cases.title: Daily news briefing for journalists
use_cases.description: Journalists need quick access to top stories across multiple beats. This skill delivers categorized article lists from ZAKER for efficient news gathering.

use_cases.title: Personalized news updates for professionals
use_cases.description: Professionals who follow specific industries can use this skill to get focused news without scanning general news feeds.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch technology news
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the latest technology news from ZAKER.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - User wants quick access to tech news without specifying details

prompt_templates.title: Browse finance category
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the top finance news articles from ZAKER.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - User requests a specific category

prompt_templates.title: Context-aware category query
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the latest news about AI and the electric vehicle industry?
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - User queries with domain context that maps to categories

prompt_templates.title: Multi-category comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the latest news for both technology and finance categories, then highlight any overlapping themes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - User requests multiple categories with cross-analysis

output_examples.input: Show me the latest technology news.
output: AI companies announce new large language models at developer conference (Tech Reporter)
Electric vehicle sales exceed expectations in Q2 market report (Auto News)
Cloud computing revenue grows across major providers (Cloud Weekly)
Central bank adjusts interest rate policy for new quarter (Finance Desk)
Stock market reacts to trade agreement updates (Market Watch)
Startup funding reaches record levels in tech sector (Venture Daily)

best_practices: Specify the news category clearly for more accurate results, such as technology, finance, or sports.
Use follow-up requests to drill deeper into a specific category after receiving initial results.
Combine with other ZAKER skills for a comprehensive news workflow including top headlines and category news.
anti_patterns: Do not expect real-time breaking news. The API returns curated trending articles, not live feeds.
Do not rely on this skill for non-Chinese news sources. ZAKER primarily serves Chinese-language content.
Do not request categories outside the nine supported options. Custom or undefined categories will not return results.

faq.question: What news categories does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports nine categories: entertainment, automotive, sports, technology, domestic news, international news, military, finance, and internet.

faq.question: How many articles does each request return?
faq.answer: Each API call returns 20 articles, sorted by publish time in descending order.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key or authentication?
faq.answer: No. The ZAKER category API is publicly accessible and does not require authentication.

faq.question: Can I search for specific topics within a category?
faq.answer: No. This skill fetches trending articles by category only. It does not support keyword search or topic filtering within categories.

faq.question: What language is the news content in?
faq.answer: ZAKER primarily serves Chinese-language content. Article titles, summaries, and authors are in Chinese.

faq.question: How is this skill different from a general news skill?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on category-based news from ZAKER with precise vertical classification. It is optimized for users who want structured news by industry rather than general top headlines.
